figures.caption: Figure 7. Visualization of input (multi-view images), output (multi-view depth maps and confidence maps) and pseudo labels (multi-view correspondences, monocular depth maps) of Reliev3R.
Figure 6. Visualization of input (multi-view images), output (multi-view depth maps and confidence maps) and pseudo labels (multi-view correspondences, monocular depth maps) of Reliev3R.

Figure 2. Similar to prior FFRMs, Reliev3R performs geometry reconstruction in a single forward pass given a group of images. However, the supervision of Reliev3R doesn’t depend on any multl-view geometric annotations (e.g., ground truth of point maps and camera poses which are registered in 3D world coordinate). Specifically, instead of directly predicting the point maps registered in world coordinate, Reliev3R predicts view-wise depth maps, which are regularized with pseudo relative depth. To learn the registration of depth maps and camera poses in world coordinate, Reliev3R draws supervision signal from pseudo image correspondences. Pseudo annotations used by Reliev3R are produced with pretrained expert models (see Sec. 4.1). Experiments in Sec. 4 demonstrate that Reliev3R catches up with and even surpasses some of FFRMs supervised with multi-view geometric annotations.
Figure 5. Visualization of input (multi-view images), output (multi-view depth maps and confidence maps) and pseudo labels (multi-view correspondences, monocular depth maps) of Reliev3R.

Figure 4. Visualization of point maps for zero-shot evaluation on ScanNet++ dataset [4], which has a different focal length from DL3DV-10K dataest [16]. Reliev3R holds its advantage over AnyCam [33] even in zero-shot circumstance. Furthermore, Reliev3R presents a notable zero-shot performance on par with fully-supervised π3† training on the same datasets.

Figure 3. Point map and camera pose visualization on DL3DV-benchmark dataset [16]. As shown above in the figure, Reliev3R delivers a visually comparable construction accuracy with the π3 [32] while surpassing FLARE [43] in the overall performance. The camera pose of FLARE above is adopted directly from the model prediction instead of solved with PnP [6] based on point maps.
Figure 8. Visualization of input (multi-view images), output (multi-view depth maps and confidence maps) and pseudo labels (multi-view correspondences, monocular depth maps) of Reliev3R.
